vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of advanced degradation. The registry-listed specimens (P1-P4) are largely absent or non-viable. The white rabbit anchor is missing from the current frame.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot is empty/soil only)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; no botanical activity.
plant_audit.P2: Empty black pot; no botanical activity.
plant_audit.P3: Empty black pot; white rabbit anchor removed.
plant_audit.P4: Empty black pot; no botanical activity.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Desiccated, dark, and devoid of organic growth.
biome_observations.debris: Persistent eggshell fragments in the primary black pot; paper receipt debris in the background.
biome_observations.anomalies: Total loss of all biological specimens over the 5-day sequence.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single leaf remnant and rabbit anchor.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Gradual loss of structural integrity; leaf chlorosis and wilting observed.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Complete disappearance of all plant matter; total systemic collapse.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has reached a terminal state. There is zero evidence of living plant tissue. The environment is currently a sterile soil-and-debris container.
vision_report.anomalies: The removal of the white rabbit anchor suggests human intervention or clearing of the desk space.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a total loss of the botanical collection. Over the observed period, the specimens transitioned from a state of decline to complete removal. The current state is a collection of empty pots containing only soil and incidental debris (eggshells). No further botanical monitoring is possible as the biome is extinct.
